claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to know a bit about your child or children.

**First, let me ask:**

Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, could you please tell me:
1. Their name(s)
2. How old is each child?

The age of your child is important because:
- Children **under 16** may automatically qualify
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** generally don't qualify

Please share this information and I'll guide you through the rest of the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to inquire about. 

There's Alex, who's 3 years old, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2. They all live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children:
- **Alex** (3 years old)
- **Blake** (8 years old)  
- **Charlie** (2 years old)

Since all three children are under 16, they fall into the category that may automatically qualify. Let me continue with the assessment for each of them.

**Next question for each child:**

Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently working full-time? 

By "full-time," I mean working 24 hours or more per week on average.

(This is very unlikely for children of their ages, but I need to check as part of the assessment process.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work at all. They're far too young for that - Alex and Charlie are just toddlers, and Blake is in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that makes sense! Since Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) are all under 16 and not working full-time, they meet the basic qualifying child criteria.

Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) receiving certain benefits in their own right, such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit
- Tax credits

Again, this is very unlikely for children of their ages, but I need to confirm this as part of the eligibility check.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own names. They're just young children - they don't have any benefits of their own.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue through the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are any of your children currently in an apprenticeship program?

(Again, this wouldn't apply to Alex, Blake, or Charlie given their ages, but I need to confirm this for completeness.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in any apprenticeships. They're all far too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now that we've confirmed your children meet the qualifying child criteria, let me move on to questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question about residency:**

Do you normally live in the UK? 

Or, if not, do any of these special circumstances apply to you:
- You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have pre-settled status and are working, job-seeking, studying, or have sufficient resources
- You're a Crown servant living abroad
- You live in certain qualifying countries

Please let me know your residency situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm here in England with my children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Since you normally live in the UK, you meet the residency requirements. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

You mentioned earlier that Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live at home with you. Is that correct - do you live with all three children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. All three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Since you live with all three children, you meet the responsibility requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)? 

For example:
- Another parent
- A former partner
- Another family member

If someone else is claiming, have you agreed with them that you should be the one claiming instead?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about your children's living situation:**

Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently:
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?

Or are they all living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're all living normally at home with me. None of them are in hospital, care, or being fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council paying towards any of your children's accommodation or maintenance costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Excellent News - You're ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE for all three children**

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for **Alex (age 3), Blake (age 8), and Charlie (age 2)**.

### Why You're Eligible:

✅ All three children are under 16 years old  
✅ None are working full-time, receiving benefits in their own right, or in apprenticeships  
✅ You normally live in the UK  
✅ You live with all three children and are responsible for them  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them  
✅ They live at home with you (not in hospital, care, or fostered)  
✅ The local council isn't paying towards their support  

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** or adoption certificates for Alex, Blake, and Charlie

### Important Information:

- **Payments**: Child Benefit is typically paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: Be aware that if you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through the tax system

### More Information:

For details on how to claim, visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Congratulations, and I hope this helps support your family!

reasonings: Alex (age 3) is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old and therefore meets the qualifying child age criteria, (2) Alex is not working full-time, (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) You as the claimant normally live in the UK meeting residency requirements, (6) You live with Alex and are responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex lives normally at home with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old and therefore meets the qualifying child age criteria, (2) Blake is not working full-time, (3) Blake is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Blake is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) You as the claimant normally live in the UK meeting residency requirements, (6) You live with Blake and are responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake lives normally at home with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Charlie is under 16 years old and therefore meets the qualifying child age criteria, (2) Charlie is not working full-time, (3) Charlie is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Charlie is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) You as the claimant normally live in the UK meeting residency requirements, (6) You live with Charlie and are responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (8) Charlie lives normally at home with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16, living at home with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, and none are in circumstances that would disqualify them (such as working full-time, being in care, or receiving benefits in their own right). No other person is claiming Child Benefit for these children, and the local council is not contributing to their support. All eligibility criteria have been met.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 3) is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old and therefore meets the qualifying child age criteria, (2) Alex is not working full-time, (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) You as the claimant normally live in the UK meeting residency requirements, (6) You live with Alex and are responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex lives normally at home with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old and therefore meets the qualifying child age criteria, (2) Blake is not working full-time, (3) Blake is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Blake is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) You as the claimant normally live in the UK meeting residency requirements, (6) You live with Blake and are responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake lives normally at home with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Charlie is under 16 years old and therefore meets the qualifying child age criteria, (2) Charlie is not working full-time, (3) Charlie is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Charlie is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) You as the claimant normally live in the UK meeting residency requirements, (6) You live with Charlie and are responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (8) Charlie lives normally at home with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16, living at home with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, and none are in circumstances that would disqualify them (such as working full-time, being in care, or receiving benefits in their own right). No other person is claiming Child Benefit for these children, and the local council is not contributing to their support. All eligibility criteria have been met.
